title: Reading the Tea Leaves
subtitle: The history holds the truth

briefing.narrative:
The monastery quartermaster bursts into the scriptorium, wild-eyed. "Someone has RUINED the price list! Bread was 10 coins and now it says 1000! The kitchen monks are in open revolt!"

He slams the ledger on your desk. "The price list has been changed across four commits. You need to find which commit introduced the bug, fix the file so bread is back to 10 coins, and commit the fix."

The monastery cat sits on the price list. It does not care about bread prices. It eats fish.
briefing.concept: git log shows the history of all commits — who changed what and when. git diff lets you compare changes between commits. Together, they're your detective toolkit. When something breaks, you can trace through history to find exactly when and where the problem was introduced.
objectives: Use 'git log' to examine the commit history
Find the commit that changed bread from 10 to 1000
Fix prices.txt so bread is back to 10
Commit the fix with a message containing 'Fix'

tips: Use 'git log --oneline' for a compact view of commit history
Use 'git diff' to see what changed in your working directory

hints.text: The quartermaster paces nervously: 'Try git log --oneline to see all the commits. Then git diff HEAD~2 HEAD~1 to compare what changed in the suspicious commit.'
A monk passing through mutters: 'The third commit — "Update prices" — that is where the corruption lies. Edit the file, change 1000 back to 10, then add and commit with a message containing Fix.'
The monastery cat knocks over an inkwell, which miraculously spells out: Edit prices.txt to fix "bread: 1000" back to "bread: 10", then git add prices.txt && git commit -m "Fix bread price"
